min_lot_size_sqft.field_value_text: Minimum lot area: 2 acres for residential development in the A-1 district.
min_lot_size_sqft.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County is one of the most restrictive suburban VA counties with large lot minimums.

height_limit_ft.field_value_text: No building or structure shall exceed 35 feet in height.
height_limit_ft.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has restrictive height limits reflecting rural character.

density_limit_units_per_acre.field_value_text: Maximum residential density: 3 dwelling units per acre.
density_limit_units_per_acre.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has very low density limits restricting multifamily development.

parking_min_spaces_per_unit.field_value_text: Required off-street parking: 2.5 spaces per dwelling unit for multifamily buildings.
parking_min_spaces_per_unit.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has high parking requirements reflecting car-dependent character.

setback_front_ft.field_value_text: Minimum front yard: 50 feet from the right-of-way.
setback_front_ft.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has large setback requirements typical of rural/exurban counties.

setback_side_ft.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has large setback requirements.

setback_rear_ft.reasoning: Synthetic value — Stafford County has large setback requirements.

discretionary_review_required.reasoning: Multifamily development requires a special use permit in Stafford County, the most restrictive category.
